text:
Market today: Tech had one of its worst weeks in a year. The real driver is a double hit — AI financing doubts (Oracle's collapse is the canary) plus a U.S. strike on Iran spiking oil and risk-off sentiment. Your chip/tech concentration is directly in the crosshairs.

Your holdings: AVGO is your biggest problem — down 16% and on SELL, margin amplifies every tick lower, stop at 336 is your line. PLTR is down 13% and also flagged SELL — no obvious near-term catalyst to bail it out. SPAL is down 17% in a 2x leveraged ETF, which decays daily and bleeds fast in choppy tape — tiny position but watch it closely. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no trading history; treat it as fragile. Margin means these losses compound fast.

Watch: 1) Oil — if Iran escalation continues, risk-off pressure hits tech harder. 2) Whether AI spend narratives stabilize — Oracle's collapse suggests the market is losing patience with AI capex stories.

Bottom line: Respect your stops on AVGO and PLTR — margin turns manageable losses into serious ones if you hesitate.
